transcript.clean_text: the stunning walled city of kakasan in the southwest of france hosted the start of stage 14 with the tour moving on into the pyrenees a couple of non-starters to tell you about surhan khan anderson unable to take his bike warren barghill neither after crashes the day before 183.7 kilometers for this stage plenty of categorized climbing to be done up and down all day a real stage for the attackers on paper and it was on the way to a first ever stage finish in kiyon things took a long long time to settle down a first group of five riders managed to get up the road squinch ricketts and chevalier were there but it took over a hundred kilometers for things to take any sort of shape up until the intermediate sprint things were battled out and that first group of five would come back together it was once the bunch began climbing that things began to separate again pulls woods and cattano away and the fight for the polkadot jersey was also on with naido quintana having missed the move behind another group of riders was coming across and this would be the foundation of the day's breakaway 14 riders getting up the road eventually and a gap of up to five and six minutes at times opening up at this point it was a three-minute gap but it looked as though the break was going to have its chance to take the stage paul's again beating woods over the top of another king of the mountain summit and then woods beaten by the corner down on the ground but thankfully for the race not out he'd chase back on take a few kilometers to do so but not before balkan molemer went off the front attacking on a descent quickly picking up half a minute of an advantage but with one very difficult categorized climb to come there was also another factor at play because guillaume martin had been moving up in the general classification he was about to take five minutes back and move up above rig alberto ran into second place ef education nippo put their riders to the front but as soon as monomer went over the top it looked as though the stage was going to belong to him inside the five final kilometers marta seemingly on his way up the gc moleman knew he was on his way to a second tour de france stage victory four years after his first at le point valley in the massive central victorious in the pyrenees three years since trek segafedo's last tour win from john denkov in ruby to balcomoloma in kyo a superb win and over a minute of a gap taken out by the flying dutchman second place will be fought out between comrade andy gita and behind guild mata coming in and waiting for the clock to tick all the glory on the day going to balcomoloma a brilliant victory trek segafredo had looked for this since the start of the tour finally on day 14 they had their award two weeks after the start malama taking the victory the yellow jersey coming in at almost seven minutes and that meaning that the gc would change in so much that well poganchad kept his yellow jersey but guillaume would rise up into second four minutes four seconds the gap between bogatyar and mata uran and third vinegar down to fourth and cathanno's ride also just about taking him into the top ten the new king of the mountains would be michael woods an arrow four-point lead over naido quintana what pulls five behind with fanart and molema in fourth and fifth so what's next well the last day before the second rest day and heading deep into the pyrenees in fact right up into its micro state andorra the tour de france leaving france and heading to the andorran capital andorra la vega some big climbs 191 kilometers and a real real mountain stage the highest point of the tour it's on its way who's going to be at the top of the time sheets will it still be pogacad or can anybody challenge him tune in to find out on gcn and eurosport you
